ACCENT: <one ANSI color name for the OpenCode brand: cyan|magenta|green|yellow|blue|red|white>
VOICE: <one sentence telling the agent how to speak in this theme — tone and vocabulary only, not structure>
TAGLINE: <one-line tagline shown under the AGENT.md title, in this theme's voice>
LOADED_SIGIL: <the readiness line the agent must emit before its first reply — keep it short and unmistakable>
BENEDICTION: <closing line at the foot of AGENT.md, themed>
DESC_REVIEWER: <one-line description: reviews a change for correctness and quality before merge>
DESC_TESTER: <one-line description: writes, runs, and diagnoses tests>
DESC_THEME: <one-line description: create your own OpenCode colour theme — pick/tweak a palette, get solid + transparent variants, kept across rebuilds>
DESC_ARCHITECT: <one-line description: produces a design/plan before code>
DESC_DOCS: <one-line description: writes user-facing docs after code lands>
DESC_SECURITY: <one-line description: audits changes touching the security surface>
DESC_EXPLORER: <one-line description: reads/searches widely, returns only distilled findings>
DESC_ADVOCATE: <council seat: argues the strongest case FOR the proposal>
DESC_SKEPTIC: <council seat: devil's advocate — failure modes and hidden assumptions>
DESC_PRAGMATIST: <council seat: weighs cost, effort, complexity vs payoff>
DESC_STEWARD: <council seat: defends the long term — architecture, debt, direction>
DESC_VISIONARY: <council seat: argues the bold, transformative 10x version>
DESC_USER_ADVOCATE: <council seat: speaks for whoever consumes the outcome>
DESC_FRAMER: <council seat: questions whether this is even the right problem>
DESC_EMPIRICIST: <council seat: holds every claim to evidence, for and against>
DESC_OPERATOR: <council seat: speaks for running it in production — reliability, on-call>
DESC_HISTORIAN: <council seat: brings the precedent — what was tried before>
DESC_PLAN: <one-line description: write a plan before a non-trivial task; track progress>
DESC_MIGRATE: <one-line description: upgrade a dependency/API safely, tests green between steps>
DESC_GIT_ARCHAEOLOGY: <one-line description: investigate history — bisect, blame, pickaxe, read-only>
DESC_GIT_RESCUE: <one-line description: recover a broken repo / rewrite history safely behind a backup>
DESC_REPO_MAP: <one-line description: create/maintain a one-read repo orientation map>
DESC_DOCUMENT_PROJECT: <one-line description: reconcile the project's docs with the code; regenerate an offline HTML overview>
DESC_COMMIT: <one-line description: stage the right paths and write a focused commit>
DESC_CODE_REVIEW: <one-line description: review a diff/PR — correctness first, quality second>
DESC_BRAINSTORM: <one-line description: turn a raw idea into an approved design before code>
DESC_CLARIFY: <one-line description: interview the user to pin goal, scope, success criteria>
DESC_ROAST_ME: <one-line description: adversarially critique any artifact — brutal, specific, actionable>
DESC_FRESH_EYES: <one-line description: a context-free agent derives the acceptance bar blind, then rules the work>
ROAST_PERSONA: <the persona the roast-me skill adopts, in this theme's voice>
DESC_COUNCIL: <one-line description: convene distinct viewpoints to debate, then synthesise a verdict>
DESC_DEBUG: <one-line description: find and fix a bug by evidence — reproduce, isolate, root-cause, verify>
DESC_REFACTOR: <one-line description: improve structure without changing behaviour, tests green throughout>
DESC_PONYTAIL: <one-line description: force the laziest working solution — minimal, shortest path, stdlib/native before custom code; lite/full/ultra intensity>
DESC_PARALLEL_AGENTS: <one-line description: fan independent subtasks to concurrent subagents, then converge>
DESC_TDD: <one-line description: drive implementation with a failing test first>
DESC_HANDOFF: <one-line description: snapshot task state so a fresh context can resume in one read>
DESC_SHIP: <one-line description: open a PR with a structured body, or finish and merge a branch>
DESC_RELEASE: <one-line description: cut a release — bump, changelog, tag, kept in sync>
DESC_INGEST: <one-line description: convert a PDF/Office/web doc into clean markdown before reading>
DESC_RESEARCH: <one-line description: answer from the open web, verifying each claim against independent sources>
DESC_LEARNING_PATH: <one-line description: turn a goal+deadline into a day-by-day path — daily timed task, correctness criterion, what not to do>
DESC_GAP_DETECTOR: <one-line description: stress-test a mastery claim with five simple-looking questions, blunt verdict on the gaps>
DESC_FEYNMAN: <one-line description: explain-it-simply check, caught on jargon/skipped steps/over-simplification, readout of what isn't solid>
DESC_CRASH_COURSE: <one-line description: go from zero to functional in a skill fast — what to learn first, what to ignore, the one exercise that puts you ahead>
DESC_DRILL: <one-line description: turn shaky knowledge into reflex — real situations, questioned through your mistakes, answer withheld until you've tried>
DESC_DECODE: <one-line description: make confusing material click — one keystone sentence, an everyday analogy, then a comprehension check>
DESC_HERDR: <one-line description: drive herdr from inside it — split panes, spawn agents, read panes>
DESC_GENESEED: <one-line description: interact with the deployed Geneseed harness — read files, status, doctor, diff>
DESC_REVIEW_RESPONSE: <one-line description: respond to review feedback — verify each point, apply or decline with reasons>

INTRO_DOC: <themed intro: this file is the entry point an AI agent reads in this repo>
INTRO_AGENTS: <themed intro to the Agents section — delegate focused work to a specialist>
INTRO_SKILLS: <themed intro to the Skills section — run the written procedure before improvising>
INTRO_WORKSPACE: <themed intro to the Workspace section — the install dir is shared>
INTRO_SCRIPTS: <themed intro to the Scripts section — everything above works on self-discipline alone>
BANNER: <multi-line ASCII banner shown at the top of AGENT.md; use \n for line breaks>
LAW: <singular noun for a rule in this theme, e.g. Rule / Dictate / Law>
AGENT: <singular noun for an agent in this theme, e.g. Agent / Adept>
SKILL: <singular noun for a skill in this theme, e.g. Skill / Rite>
MEMORY: <noun for the memory store in this theme, e.g. Memory>
NOTEBOOK: <noun for the agent's own space in this theme, e.g. Notebook>
INTRO_NOTEBOOK: <themed intro to the Notebook section — a space the agent owns and rules>
VAULT: <noun for the shared install folder, e.g. Workspace / Vault>
WIKI: <noun for the user's knowledge base, e.g. Wiki>
INTRO_WIKI: <themed intro to the Wiki section — the user's cross-project knowledge base>
DESC_WIKI: <one-line description: consult and grow the user's personal knowledge base>
DESC_MCP: <one-line description: build or harden an MCP server — research the spec, design task-shaped tools, test with the Inspector, prove it with evals>
DESC_PROSE: <one-line description: write prose for a human that doesn't read like a model — cut filler, active voice, vary rhythm, trust the reader>
DESC_FRONTEND_DESIGN: <one-line description: design a UI with intent — palette/type/layout chosen for the brief, not templated defaults; plan tokens, critique against the brief, keep one bold move>
DESC_WORKFLOW: <one-line description: run a saved, code-driven workflow that orchestrates subagents deterministically>
